other_observers: Gregg Osland (12/15/20), Josh Bruenning (12/15/20), Jim Nachel (12/15/2020),
description: Large emberezid passerine uniform gray-brown color except for dull rufous crown and vent area. Pale thin eyering. Gray short conical beak. Throat and lower belly washed with buff. Dull slaty malar stripes, tail and small diffuse spots on upper chest and sides of breast. Dark eyes, legs, feet. Frequently flicked wings and tail in unison. Weak flyer. Vocalized typical low pitched screechy call note. A higher-pitched cardinal-like call note was attributed to the towhee during a short period when it seemed agitated and flewfrom its perch. Similar in size to nearby Spotted Towhees. Several other observers viewed this bird on 12/20/20 including Josh Bruenning, Skyler Bol and Rachel Hopper. On 12/22/20, observes included Shawn Mason, Dave Farmer (excellent photos in eBird), and Joe Mammoser, among others.
